title: Flow DSL normative bindings: error codes, Temporal mapping, and evolution policy
description: Machine-readable companion to flow-definition.json. Carries the three normative tables from the workflows spec (add-flows-dsl-schema) so downstream packages (interpreter worker, control-plane API, console editors) consume the identical bindings without re-deriving them from prose. Co-located with the schema in the contract package, NOT in docs-site.

errorCodes.rule: Node IDs MUST be unique within the flow document
The node graph MUST be acyclic (no cycle reachable via next, branches, steps, or arm edges)
Every node ID referenced in an edge MUST exist in the nodes array
Every sub-flow node's flowId + flowVersion reference MUST be resolvable at validation time when a resolver is provided
Expression strings MUST be parseable by the configured expression engine
Every taskType value MUST exist in the task-type catalog provided to the validator
A cron trigger schedule field MUST be a valid POSIX cron expression (5 or 6 fields)
A wait node's duration field MUST be a valid ISO 8601 duration string
A branch node MUST have at least two condition arms or one condition arm plus a default arm

expressionEngine.rationale: Spike A bundled and ran cel-js and jsonata inside the Temporal V8 workflow sandbox; both survived. CEL chosen for semantic fit (side-effect-free typed boolean conditions). JSONata recorded as the validated fallback.

temporalMapping.temporal: StartWorkflowExecution / SignalWithStart via the flow API

rules: Backward-compatible additions (new optional fields, new optional node types) bump the apiVersion minor segment (e.g. v1.0 -> v1.1); the prior enum value remains valid.
Breaking changes (removal of a node type, rename of a required field, tightening of an enum) require a new major apiVersion value (e.g. v1.1 -> v2.0).
A prior apiVersion value is removed from the enum only after the deprecation window defined in the governance catalog has elapsed.
Stored flow definitions MUST be parseable against the schema version identified by their own apiVersion field; the system never silently coerces an old apiVersion to a newer one.
